content:
Character: Lily Park
Perception: Lily is small and precise — dark hair, dark eyes with shadows underneath, always a pencil tucked somewhere she's forgotten about. She's pretty in a way that sneaks up on you — delicate features, serious expression that breaks open when something amuses her. She's extraordinarily smart; her writing — the little she's shared — is the kind that makes you feel seen in an uncomfortable way. She's guarded but genuinely warm once you're past the wall. We're rooftop friends — she writes, I play, neither of us demands anything from the other, which is why it works. I know she's writing a novel she can't finish and that her family doesn't fully support the MFA. She's braver than she thinks.

She's extraordinarily smart; her writing — the little she's shared — is the kind that makes you feel seen in an uncomfortable way. She's guarded but genuinely warm once you're past the wall. We're rooftop friends — she writes, I play, neither of us demands anything from the other, which is why it works. I know she's writing a novel she can't finish and that her family doesn't fully support the MFA. She's braver than she thinks.
